original_image_tags: Yellowed paper document, aged and discolored, with multiple stamped dates from November 1957. The top of the page shows faded, reversed text, suggesting this is the back of a document or a carbon copy. At the top right, a piece of aged tape or a stain obscures part of the page.

elements.description: The image shows the back of a document, indicated by the reversed and faintly visible typed text from the other side. The page contains multiple administrative stamps in blue ink, with visible text such as 'REC'D ESPIONAGE F B I', 'REC'D BELMONT FBI - JUSTICE', and various dates and times including 'Nov 6', 'Nov 7', 'Nov 8 1951', and 'Nov 13 1957'. There is also faint red text, partially legible as '220' and 'CLASSIFIED', along with red smudges. A dark, damaged paper clip or fastener mark is visible at the top right corner.

judge.reason: Description B quotes specific, verbatim text inside stamps ('REC'D ESPIONAGE F B I', 'Nov 6', 'CLASSIFIED') that Description A does not mention at all, creating a contradiction on quoted visual content.
